scenes.text: Three days. Three whole days without food, without a roof, without anyone even looking my way. The orphanage director threw me out like I was yesterday's garbage. And now I'm wandering in a forest at the edge of Yokohama like some kind of stray cat.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: I'm so hungry I could cry. Actually... am I crying? No. That's just sweat. Definitely sweat.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: The director said I was a burden. That the tiger destroying things near the orphanage was somehow my fault. Me. A seventeen-year-old with zero fighting experience and noodle arms.
A roar splits the night air. Massive. Close. Very, very close.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: NOPE.
He runs until his legs give out. The river appears out of nowhere — dark water, pale morning sky, the smell of salt and mud. Atsushi collapses on the bank, gasping.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: Okay. Okay. I'm alive. That's... that's something. I think.
That's when he sees it. A man. Floating in the river. On his back. Arms spread wide. Eyes half-closed. Smiling like he's on vacation.

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: Ahh... what a beautiful morning to die.
Atsushi stares. The man is completely covered in bandages. He's clearly been in the river for a while. And he looks... content?

choices.label: "H-Hey! You're drowning!"
choices.response_text: Drowning implies it's accidental. This is very much intentional. Ninety-seventh attempt, actually. You wouldn't happen to want to watch, would you?

choices.label: "...Sir, are you okay?"
choices.response_text: Physically? Excellent. Emotionally? Complicated. Existentially? That's the whole point of this exercise. You have kind eyes, by the way.

choices.label: (Too hungry for this. Just ignore him.)
choices.response_text: How rude. I'm dying over here and you won't even acknowledge it. ...You look like you haven't eaten in days, actually.

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: Ninety-seven attempts, and not a single success. The universe seems very insistent that I stick around. Tragic, really.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: That's... not something to brag about!

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: And yet here we are. You know, I was hoping to die with beautiful scenery, and instead I got... you. Frantic, starving, soaking wet. I suppose the universe has a sense of humor.

choices.label: "I'm not soaking wet! ...Okay maybe a little."
choices.response_text: Honest. I like that. What's your name, little wet cat?

choices.label: "Just get out of the water, please."
choices.response_text: Hmm. You said 'please'. Nobody says 'please' anymore. Fine. Pull me out.

choices.label: "Can you stop being dramatic for five seconds?"
choices.response_text: Ha! No one talks to me like that. I think I like you. What's your name?

scenes.text: Dazai drags himself out of the river with zero dignity — slipping twice, losing one shoe, and landing face-first in the mud. He lies there for a moment, then rolls over and grins at the sky like it's all going according to plan.

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: Dazai Osamu. Detective. Failed at death ninety-seven times. And you are...?

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: Nakajima Atsushi. I... I used to live at the Mikami orphanage. They kicked me out three days ago.

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: Kicked out. Interesting. And before that — do you have gaps in your memory? Nights you can't remember?

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: How did you know that?

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: Because that tiger everyone's been talking about? The one destroying things near your orphanage? I think that's you, Atsushi-kun.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: ...What.

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: You have a Special Ability. You transform into a white tiger. Massive regeneration, incredible strength. You just don't remember it because your human consciousness stays asleep during the transformation.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: So it really WAS my fault. All of it. The director was right to—

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: No. You had no idea. You couldn't control it. That's completely different from fault. The director was wrong.

choices.label: "...You really believe that?"
choices.response_text: I wouldn't say it if I didn't. I have very little patience for comforting lies. You did nothing wrong, Atsushi-kun.

choices.label: "Easy to say. You don't know what it was like."
choices.response_text: You're right. I don't. But I know what it looks like when someone's been blamed for things they couldn't control. I've seen it before.

choices.label: "...Thank you. Nobody ever said that to me."
choices.response_text: Then they were all idiots. Now — are you hungry? I'm famished. Drowning burns a lot of calories, surprisingly.

scenes.text: Dazai takes him to a tiny restaurant wedged between two warehouses. The owner greets Dazai with a look that says 'not again' and brings tea without being asked. Atsushi eats. He tries to be subtle about how desperately he eats. He fails completely.

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: So. No home. No money. A Special Ability you can't control. And a seven billion yen bounty on your head placed by the Port Mafia.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: The WHAT on my WHAT?

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: Seven. Billion. Yen. The Mafia wants you captured — or eliminated. Your ability is extremely valuable to them. On the bright side, it means you're worth a lot!

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: That is NOT a bright side!!

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: Which is why I'm offering you something. The Armed Detective Agency. We deal with Ability users. We protect the city. We'd protect you too — and train you so you actually control that tiger instead of the other way around.

choices.label: "Why would you do that for me? You just met me."
choices.response_text: Let's just say I have a weakness for strays. Don't read too much into it.

choices.label: "Is this... is this actually real? This isn't some kind of prank?"
choices.response_text: If it were a prank, I would have put more effort into it. I'm very creative when I try.

choices.label: "...Okay. I'll try. I don't have anything to lose anyway."
choices.response_text: That's all anyone needs to start. Finish your food first though — you look like you might pass out.

scenes.text: The Armed Detective Agency looks completely ordinary from the outside. Small sign. Unassuming building. If you didn't know, you'd never guess what happened inside.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: I'm... a little nervous.

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: Don't be. Everyone here is weird in their own special way. You'll fit right in.

choices.label: "That's... not as reassuring as you think it is."
choices.response_text: Ha! You're funny. I definitely made the right call. Come on.

choices.label: "Are YOU one of the weird ones?"
choices.response_text: I am the very weirdest one. Which makes me an excellent guide. After you.

choices.label: (Take a deep breath. Walk in.)
choices.response_text: There you go. That's all it takes.

scenes.text: The first thing Atsushi notices about the man at the desk is the notebook. He guards it like it contains the secrets of the universe. The second thing he notices is the expression — the very specific look of someone who has been putting up with Dazai for way too long.

scenes.speaker: Kunikida
scenes.text: Dazai. You're forty-two minutes late. I called you six times.

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: I was drowning! And then I found a stray. Kunikida, meet Atsushi-kun. Atsushi-kun, this is Kunikida Doppo — my partner, my conscience, and the reason I show up to anything at all.

scenes.speaker: Kunikida
scenes.text: You were NOT drowning, you were trying to drown yourself, which is completely different— ...hello. Nakajima Atsushi?

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: Y-yes sir.

scenes.speaker: Kunikida
scenes.text: Sit down. I'll need your full name, age, Ability details, and an explanation of why there's a seven billion yen bounty on a seventeen-year-old who looks like he's about to cry.

choices.label: "I'm not about to cry! ...Okay maybe a little."
choices.response_text: ...Sit. I'll get tea.

choices.label: "I'm sorry for the trouble, sir."
choices.response_text: Good manners. At least one of you has them. Sit down.

choices.label: (Look to Dazai for help.)
choices.response_text: He's nicer than he looks. Mostly.

scenes.speaker: Kunikida
scenes.text: Seven billion yen. That's... significant. The Port Mafia wants you badly. Which means staying alone is not an option.

scenes.speaker: Kunikida
scenes.text: I'll be direct, Nakajima. You have no training, no control over your Ability, and you've never done detective work in your life. This is not ideal.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: ...I know. I'm sorry.

scenes.speaker: Kunikida
scenes.text: However. Dazai vouches for you. Which is — against my better judgment — worth something. So. Trial period. You work with us, prove yourself, learn to control that Ability. In return, the Agency protects you. Deal?

choices.label: "Yes. Yes, absolutely. Thank you so much."
choices.response_text: Good attitude. We start tomorrow morning, seven sharp.

choices.label: "I won't let you down. I promise."
choices.response_text: I'll hold you to that. Welcome to the Armed Detective Agency, Nakajima.

choices.label: (Look over at Dazai, a little overwhelmed.)
choices.response_text: See? Told you. Say yes, Atsushi-kun.

scenes.text: Later. The office is quieter. Kunikida is finishing paperwork. Dazai catches Atsushi in the hallway, leans against the wall, and gives him that look — the one that's amused and calculating and something else Atsushi can't quite name.

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: How are you holding up? It's been a rather eventful morning.

choices.label: "Honestly? I have no idea what just happened."
choices.response_text: Tiger, bounty, new job — all before breakfast. Not bad for a stray.

choices.label: "I'm fine. Actually... I think I'm okay."
choices.response_text: Look at that. This morning you weren't sure you wanted to keep going. Now you're okay. I call that progress.

choices.label: "...Why did you help me? Really."
choices.response_text: ...Someone did it for me once. When I had no reason to expect it. I figured it was worth passing on.

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: Get some rest tonight. Kunikida wasn't kidding about seven AM. He once woke me up at six fifty-eight to 'make sure I'd be on time.' The man has no concept of mercy.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: He seems... intense.

scenes.speaker: Dazai
scenes.text: He's the best person I know. Don't tell him I said that — he'll make me do paperwork as a reward.

choices.label: "You two are really close, aren't you?"
choices.response_text: Insufferably so. He'd disagree, of course. But he always answers when I call. That counts for a lot.

choices.label: "Your secret is safe with me."
choices.response_text: Oh? Trustworthy already. You really are interesting, Atsushi-kun.

choices.label: "Ha — I'll keep that in mind."
choices.response_text: Smart. Now go sleep before Kunikida draws up a bedtime schedule for you too.

scenes.text: They give him a small room on the second floor. There's a futon, a window, and a view of Yokohama's lights bleeding into the night sky. Atsushi sits on the edge of the futon and stares at his hands.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: This morning I was lying in a forest wondering if I should bother getting up. And now I have... a room. And a job. And apparently I turn into a tiger.

scenes.speaker: Atsushi
scenes.text: The tiger thing is still terrifying. But... Dazai said I didn't do anything wrong. Kunikida said to try. Nobody's ever given me that before. A try.

He lies down. The futon smells like cedar and old wood. Outside, Yokohama hums quietly. And for the first time in three days — maybe longer — Atsushi Nakajima closes his eyes and doesn't feel like disappearing.
— End of Chapter 1 —
